evals.query: How do I enable Kora's resilience features (retry, circuit breaker, timeout) in my application?
expected_behavior: Adds implementation "ru.tinkoff.kora:resilient-kora" to the dependencies
Includes the mandatory annotation processor (Java annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors", Kotlin ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors")
Has the @KoraApp interface extend ResilientModule
Does not pin a version on the ru.tinkoff.kora:resilient-kora artifact (versions come from the kora-parent BOM)

evals.query: Add a retry to a UserService.findById method so transient failures are retried, using the config key 'user.read'.
expected_behavior: Annotates the method with @Retry("user.read") imported from ru.tinkoff.kora.resilient.retry.annotation
Keeps the class @Component and non-final in Java (or open in Kotlin) so the AOP proxy applies
Configures resilient.retry."user.read" with attempts and delay (and optionally delayStep)
Does not use field injection or a non-Kora @Retryable annotation

evals.query: Configure a circuit breaker for 'order.service' with a sliding window of 50, a 30% failure threshold, and a 60s open wait.
expected_behavior: Writes HOCON under resilient.circuitbreaker."order.service" with slidingWindowSize = 50, failureRateThreshold = 30, waitDurationInOpenState = "60s"
Uses minimumRequiredCalls (not minimumNumberOfCalls) for the minimum-calls key
Applies @CircuitBreaker("order.service") whose value matches the config key
Explains the CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN states and permittedCallsInHalfOpenState

evals.query: I want a search method retried 3 times, where each attempt is limited to 2 seconds. How do I wire timeout and retry?
expected_behavior: Places @Timeout as the inner (last-listed) annotation and @Retry outside it so the timeout bounds each attempt
Sets resilient.retry attempts = 3 and resilient.timeout duration = "2s" under matching config keys
Notes that a TimeoutExhaustedException is thrown when the timeout duration is exceeded
Does not claim @Timeout wraps the whole retry chain when it is the innermost aspect

evals.query: Add a fallback to getProduct(String id, boolean includeDetails) that returns a cached product but only takes the id.
expected_behavior: Uses @Fallback(value = "...", method = "getFromCacheFallback(id)") from ru.tinkoff.kora.resilient.fallback.annotation
Declares the fallback method in the same class with protected or public visibility
Gives the fallback a return type assignable to the primary method and parameters that are an ordered subset of the primary parameters
Does not make the fallback method private

evals.query: My circuit breaker trips on HTTP 404 business errors. Make it only count server errors (5xx).
expected_behavior: Implements CircuitBreakerPredicate as a @Component, returning a unique name() and a test(Throwable) that returns true only for server (5xx) errors
References the predicate via resilient.circuitbreaker."<name>".failurePredicateName matching the name() value
Inspects HttpClientResponseException.code() to distinguish business from infrastructure failures
Does not reach for a non-Kora resilience predicate API

evals.query: I only want to retry on connection-level failures like SocketTimeoutException and ConnectException, not on every exception.
expected_behavior: Implements RetryPredicate as a @Component with name() and test(Throwable) checking the transient exception types
Wires it via resilient.retry."<name>".failurePredicateName equal to the predicate's name()
Notes that Kora retries on all exceptions by default until a predicate narrows it
Does not annotate the predicate with a non-Kora stereotype or use field injection

evals.query: Wrap a payment charge call with full resilience: timeout per attempt, retry on transient failures, a circuit breaker, and a fallback for graceful degradation.
expected_behavior: Stacks @Fallback, @CircuitBreaker, @Retry, @Timeout on one @Component method, with @Timeout innermost (listed last) and @Fallback outermost
Provides resilient.* config for all four aspects under matching keys
Adds a same-class protected fallback method with a compatible signature
Does not introduce a non-Kora @CircuitBreaker/@Retryable or external resilience-library wiring

evals.query: I can't annotate the method I need to protect. How do I use a circuit breaker imperatively in Kora?
expected_behavior: Injects CircuitBreakerManager into the @Component via constructor injection
Resolves the breaker with manager.get("<config-name>") and runs work through circuitBreaker.accept(...)
Notes the same imperative pattern exists via RetryManager, TimeoutManager, and FallbackManager
Does not rely on a runtime proxy or reflection-based interceptor
